evidence_excerpt: CT Abd/Pelvis reported 'mild interval worsening of numerous hepatic masses, with interval worsening of large volume ascites' and concern for partial gastric outlet obstruction; patient discharged to hospice-level care with failure to thrive.

quality_of_life.functional_status_evidence_excerpt: Discharge condition: Activity Status 'Out of Bed with assistance to chair or wheelchair,' indicating major dependence; hospice-level care.
quality_of_life.symptom_burden_evidence_excerpt: Ongoing partial gastric outlet obstruction and large-volume ascites; discharged with morphine and multiple antiemetics; instructions emphasize comfort care via hospice.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: CT Abd/Pelvis: 'Mild interval worsening of numerous hepatic masses, with interval worsening of large volume ascites' and concern for partial gastric outlet obstruction.
Left lower extremity ultrasound: 'Deep vein thrombosis involving the left common femoral, proximal femoral vein, distal femoral vein, popliteal vein, and posterior tibial and peroneal veins.'
Paracentesis performed: discharge instructions state 'You had a procedure to drain fluid from your abdomen'; ascitic fluid studies documented.
Treatment initiated for DVT: 'You were given a blood thinner because of a blood clot in your leg.'
Transition to hospice indicated: 'Take your medications unless directed otherwise by your hospice provider,' reflecting decline and comfort-focused care.

notes_on_uncertainty: No post-discharge follow-up is provided; long-term outcome and quality-of-life categories are inferred from in-hospital progression and transition to hospice. Exact diagnosis date is not stated, so admission date was used as the index reference.
